notes: The fiber/coarea and relative-chain backbone is established. The only obstruction is that item (3), as stated with only y3 central, is false; the correct lower bound depends on distance to the full base boundary, and yields the desired S1 S2 S3 lower bound on a fixed positive fraction of the central base.

task_title: Hard mixed-exponent obstruction beyond pointwise filling
notes: The hard part has been reduced to a precise actual-fiber estimate: prove that the degree-carrying measure dmu=(sigma2/sigma1)dx sees average sigma1^2 at least sqrt(S3/S2). This is the point where a solid-torus or relative Steenrod-square argument should enter.

The natural Steenrod-square route on the ordinary mod-2 relative 2-cycle space cannot supply the missing sqrt(S3/S2) obstruction. A stronger invariant must remember fillings, linking, connected/degree-one sheet structure, or the actual mixed-Jacobian structure of fibers of F=(f3,f4).

task_title: Direct proof of the base-stretch dispersion lemma
notes: The raw sigma_1^2-average over total mu should be replaced by a dispersion estimate for a degree-carrying submeasure nu0 derived from dnu=min(1,sigma_1^{-2})J_2F dx. Proving the stated low-stretch exclusion for nu0 would imply the original theorem with the desired mixed exponent. The remaining hard step is the solid-torus/framing obstruction showing that the selected degree-one sheet cannot mostly lie where sigma_1^2 << sqrt(S3/S2).

task_title: Direct analytic proof of the fiber dispersion or a sharp obstruction to it
notes: The directional-coarea attack proves all non-cylinder regimes and isolates the exact obstruction: long thin relative cylinders with Fill ~ Area^2/R1. Such cycles satisfy all numerical directional and monomial estimates, so the mixed exponent requires a genuinely global actual-fiber argument. The remaining necessary lemma is a coherent-sheet low-stretch exclusion: a degree-carrying sheet over P x B0 cannot mostly be selected from regions with sigma1^2 << sqrt(S3/S2) when R1 and R3R4 are both too small.

task_title: Recover the original Guth thesis proof or a faithful solid-torus reconstruction
